reviews.review_msg: His PowerPoints don't spell out Greek words you get tested on. A majority of the class failed the midterm and he wants full essays in discussion posts. Adequately preparing for exams is difficult even with effort and he takes no responsibility for the classes poor grade.
doesn't give any guidelines on any test or assignment he just expects everyone to know. very rude as well
Very vague on assignment rubrics and has very boring lectures. Such a tough grader on tests and doesn't respond to emails.
dont take this class, he really just doesn't give clear instructions.

Professor Krallis was very passionate but his lectures were information heavy and difficult to follow. They were 2 hours full of information, that was difficult to remember and understand. His exams could be on anything from his lectures. My advice for the tests is to keep studying all the study questions for each week and keep up with readings.
He is too harsh with grading the exams. Though, the lectures seem to be interesting. This course is unique in which six different profs are lecturing in it.

lectures were fun but kinda hard to understand. worst part of class was during the final exam the etchers were talking during it, not a whisper but a conversation volume. unclear instructors for the essay, there was no rubric and my feedback on it was " did not follow rubric" but there was no rubric other than the powerpoint which I followed

Fun lectures, but lacks clarity for assignments-- this evidently dropped my grade because he didn't explain what he is looking for even when I asked. He also never explained about his "quiz" portion of the class, nor did he say when they'll be up. Syllabus says quizzes are 24 hours long, but they're until midnight the same day.
very knowledgeable and passionate about his topics. wants you to actually read all of his readings but is always accessible during office hours to talk.
A lot of readings, but they are very relevant to the discussions and exams.
